force_refresh.description: Invalidate cache and fetch fresh data

cache_timeout.description: Cache timeout override in seconds (0 to disable)

saved_metric.description: If true, 'name' refers to a saved metric from the dataset (use get_dataset_info to see available metrics). When set, 'aggregate' is ignored.

sql_expression.description: Custom SQL aggregate expression for an adhoc metric, e.g. 'COUNT(CASE WHEN closed_won THEN 1 END)::numeric / NULLIF(COUNT(*),0)'. Metric-only â mutually exclusive with 'name', 'aggregate', and 'saved_metric'. Requires 'label'.

x.description: X-axis column. If omitted, defaults to the dataset's primary datetime column (main_dttm_col).

saved_metric.description: If true, 'name' refers to a saved metric from the dataset (use get_dataset_info to see available metrics). When set, 'aggregate' is ignored.

sql_expression.description: Custom SQL aggregate expression for an adhoc metric, e.g. 'COUNT(CASE WHEN closed_won THEN 1 END)::numeric / NULLIF(COUNT(*),0)'. Metric-only â mutually exclusive with 'name', 'aggregate', and 'saved_metric'. Requires 'label'.

time_grain.description: PT1S, PT1M, PT1H, P1D, P1W, P1M, P3M, P1Y

saved_metric.description: If true, 'name' refers to a saved metric from the dataset (use get_dataset_info to see available metrics). When set, 'aggregate' is ignored.

sql_expression.description: Custom SQL aggregate expression for an adhoc metric, e.g. 'COUNT(CASE WHEN closed_won THEN 1 END)::numeric / NULLIF(COUNT(*),0)'. Metric-only â mutually exclusive with 'name', 'aggregate', and 'saved_metric'. Requires 'label'.

x_axis_time_format.description: Date format for temporal x-axis labels (e.g. 'smart_date', '%Y-%m-%d'). Only applies when the x-axis column is temporal.

show_value.description: Show data labels on each data point

currency_format.description: Currency symbol and placement applied to numeric values.

symbol_position.description: Whether to render the symbol before or after the value

color_scheme.description: Superset color scheme ID (e.g. 'supersetColors', 'lyftColors', 'googleCategory10c', 'd3Category10'). When omitted, Superset's default scheme is used.

op.description: LIKE/ILIKE use % wildcards. IN/NOT IN take a list.

value.description: For IN/NOT IN, provide a list.

filters.description: Structured filters (column/op/value). Do NOT use adhoc_filters or raw SQL expressions.

series_limit.description: Max number of series to show when group_by is set. Limits the distinct values rendered as separate lines/bars. Only applies when group_by is specified.

query_mode.description: Query mode: 'raw' returns individual rows without aggregation, 'aggregate' groups data using metrics. When set to 'raw', all columns are treated as plain columns regardless of any aggregate settings. Defaults to auto-detection: 'raw' if no column has an aggregate function, 'aggregate' otherwise.

saved_metric.description: If true, 'name' refers to a saved metric from the dataset (use get_dataset_info to see available metrics). When set, 'aggregate' is ignored.

sql_expression.description: Custom SQL aggregate expression for an adhoc metric, e.g. 'COUNT(CASE WHEN closed_won THEN 1 END)::numeric / NULLIF(COUNT(*),0)'. Metric-only â mutually exclusive with 'name', 'aggregate', and 'saved_metric'. Requires 'label'.

value.description: For IN/NOT IN, provide a list.

filters.description: Structured filters (column/op/value). Do NOT use adhoc_filters or raw SQL expressions.

anyOf.description:
Sort specification with explicit direction.

Accepts either this object or a bare column-name string in `sort_by`
lists. Bare strings default to descending, which matches the
sort-by-metric "top N" pattern most commonly used for tables.

ascending.description: Sort ascending. Defaults to False (descending) to match the typical sort-by-metric top-N use case.

sort_by.description: Columns to sort by. Each entry is either a column-name string (defaults to descending) or a SortByConfig object with explicit `ascending`. Descending matches the sort-by-metric top-N pattern most common for tables.

color_scheme.description: Superset color scheme ID applied to conditional/cell formatting (e.g. 'supersetColors').

saved_metric.description: If true, 'name' refers to a saved metric from the dataset (use get_dataset_info to see available metrics). When set, 'aggregate' is ignored.

sql_expression.description: Custom SQL aggregate expression for an adhoc metric, e.g. 'COUNT(CASE WHEN closed_won THEN 1 END)::numeric / NULLIF(COUNT(*),0)'. Metric-only â mutually exclusive with 'name', 'aggregate', and 'saved_metric'. Requires 'label'.

metric.description: Value metric (use aggregate e.g. SUM, COUNT for ad-hoc, or set saved_metric=True for a saved dataset metric)

show_value.description: Show data labels on each data point

color_scheme.description: Superset color scheme ID (e.g. 'supersetColors', 'lyftColors'). When omitted, Superset's default scheme is used.

currency_format.description: Currency symbol and placement applied to numeric values.

symbol_position.description: Whether to render the symbol before or after the value

currency_format_secondary.description: Currency symbol and placement applied to numeric values.

op.description: LIKE/ILIKE use % wildcards. IN/NOT IN take a list.

value.description: For IN/NOT IN, provide a list.

filters.description: Structured filters (column/op/value). Do NOT use adhoc_filters or raw SQL expressions.

chart_type.description: Chart type discriminator - MUST be 'handlebars' for custom HTML template charts. Handlebars charts render query results using Handlebars templates, enabling fully custom layouts like KPI cards, leaderboards, and formatted reports.

handlebars_template.description:
Handlebars HTML template string. Data is available as {{data}} array. Built-in helpers: {{dateFormat val format='YYYY-MM-DD'}}, {{formatNumber val}}, {{stringify obj}}. Example: '
    {{#each data}}
  • {{this.name}}: {{this.value}}
    • {{/each}}
'

query_mode.description: Query mode: 'aggregate' groups data with metrics, 'raw' returns individual rows

saved_metric.description: If true, 'name' refers to a saved metric from the dataset (use get_dataset_info to see available metrics). When set, 'aggregate' is ignored.

sql_expression.description: Custom SQL aggregate expression for an adhoc metric, e.g. 'COUNT(CASE WHEN closed_won THEN 1 END)::numeric / NULLIF(COUNT(*),0)'. Metric-only â mutually exclusive with 'name', 'aggregate', and 'saved_metric'. Requires 'label'.

columns.description: Columns to display in raw mode (query_mode='raw'). Each column specifies a column name to include in the query results.

saved_metric.description: If true, 'name' refers to a saved metric from the dataset (use get_dataset_info to see available metrics). When set, 'aggregate' is ignored.

sql_expression.description: Custom SQL aggregate expression for an adhoc metric, e.g. 'COUNT(CASE WHEN closed_won THEN 1 END)::numeric / NULLIF(COUNT(*),0)'. Metric-only â mutually exclusive with 'name', 'aggregate', and 'saved_metric'. Requires 'label'.

groupby.description: Columns to group by in aggregate mode (query_mode='aggregate'). These become the dimensions for aggregation.

saved_metric.description: If true, 'name' refers to a saved metric from the dataset (use get_dataset_info to see available metrics). When set, 'aggregate' is ignored.

sql_expression.description: Custom SQL aggregate expression for an adhoc metric, e.g. 'COUNT(CASE WHEN closed_won THEN 1 END)::numeric / NULLIF(COUNT(*),0)'. Metric-only â mutually exclusive with 'name', 'aggregate', and 'saved_metric'. Requires 'label'.

metrics.description: Metrics to aggregate in aggregate mode. Each must have an aggregate function (e.g., SUM, COUNT).

op.description: LIKE/ILIKE use % wildcards. IN/NOT IN take a list.

value.description: For IN/NOT IN, provide a list.

style_template.description: Optional CSS styles to apply to the rendered template

chart_type.description: Chart type discriminator - MUST be 'big_number'. Creates Big Number charts that display a single prominent metric value. Set show_trendline=True with a temporal_column for a number with trendline, or leave show_trendline=False for a standalone number.

saved_metric.description: If true, 'name' refers to a saved metric from the dataset (use get_dataset_info to see available metrics). When set, 'aggregate' is ignored.

sql_expression.description: Custom SQL aggregate expression for an adhoc metric, e.g. 'COUNT(CASE WHEN closed_won THEN 1 END)::numeric / NULLIF(COUNT(*),0)'. Metric-only â mutually exclusive with 'name', 'aggregate', and 'saved_metric'. Requires 'label'.

metric.description: The metric to display as a big number. Must include an aggregate function (e.g., SUM, COUNT).

temporal_column.description: Temporal column for the trendline x-axis. Required when show_trendline is True.

time_grain.description: Time granularity for trendline data. Common values: PT1H (hour), P1D (day), P1W (week), P1M (month), P1Y (year).

show_trendline.description: Show a trendline below the big number. Requires 'temporal_column' to be set.

subheader.description: Subtitle text displayed below the big number

y_axis_format.description: Number format string for the metric value (e.g., '$,.2f' for currency, ',.0f' for integers, '.2%' for percentages)

time_format.description: Date format string for trendline x-axis labels (e.g. 'smart_date', '%Y-%m-%d'). Only applies when show_trendline=True.

currency_format.description: Currency symbol and placement applied to numeric values.

symbol_position.description: Whether to render the symbol before or after the value

color_scheme.description: Superset color scheme ID for the trendline (e.g. 'supersetColors'). When omitted, Superset's default scheme is used.

compare_lag.description: Number of time periods to compare against. Displays a percentage change vs the prior period.

op.description: LIKE/ILIKE use % wildcards. IN/NOT IN take a list.

value.description: For IN/NOT IN, provide a list.

sanitization_warnings.description: Internal: warnings emitted when user input was altered by sanitization. Populated by the ``mode='before'`` validator before chart_name is rewritten, so the tool can surface a notice to the caller instead of silently dropping content.
